  1415→# --- Pipeline command (Feature 1) ---
  1416→
  1417→run_pipeline() {
  1418→  local REQUIREMENTS_FILE="$1"
  1419→  local WORKSPACE="${2:-}"

1417→run_pipeline() {
  1418→  local REQUIREMENTS_FILE="$1"
  1419→  local WORKSPACE="${2:-}"
  1420→  local TEAM_NAME="${3:-}"
  1421→  local DRY_RUN="${4:-false}"
  1422→
  1423→  if [ ! -f "$REQUIREMENTS_FILE" ]; then
  1424→    echo "ERROR: Requirements file not found: $REQUIREMENTS_FILE"
  1425→    exit 1
  1426→  fi
  1427→
  1428→  local REQUIREMENTS
  1429→  REQUIREMENTS=$(cat "$REQUIREMENTS_FILE")
  1430→
  1431→  # Default to first workspace/team if not specified
  1432→  if [ -z "$WORKSPACE" ]; then
  1433→    WORKSPACE=$(jq -r '.workspaces | keys[0]' "$CONFIG")
  1434→  fi
  1435→  if [ -z "$TEAM_NAME" ]; then
  1436→    TEAM_NAME=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].teams | keys[0]" "$CONFIG")
  1437→  fi
  1438→
  1439→  local API_KEY
  1440→  API_KEY=$(resolve_api_key "$WORKSPACE") || exit 1
  1441→
  1442→  log "Parsing requirements into stories..."
  1443→
  1444→  # Spawn Claude to parse requirements into structured JSON
  1445→  local PARSE_OUTPUT
  1446→  PARSE_OUTPUT=$(claude --print --max-turns 3 --output-format json -p "Parse the following product requirements into structured development stories.
  1447→
  1448→Output ONLY a JSON array with this structure:
  1449→[
  1450→  {
  1451→    \"title\": \"Short imperative title (e.g., 'Add user authentication')\",
  1452→    \"description\": \"Detailed description with acceptance criteria\",
  1453→    \"estimate\": 1,
  1454→    \"labels\": [\"feature\"],
  1455→    \"dependencies\": []
  1456→  }
  1457→]
  1458→
  1459→Rules:
  1460→- estimate is in story points (1=trivial, 2=small, 3=medium, 5=large, 8=very large)
  1461→- labels: use 'feature', 'bug', 'chore', 'improvement'
  1462→- dependencies: list titles of other stories this one depends on (must complete first)
  1463→- Break large requirements into small, independent stories
  1464→- Each story should be completable in one agent session (1-3 hours of work)
  1465→- Output ONLY the JSON array, nothing else
  1466→
  1467→## Requirements
  1468→
  1469→${REQUIREMENTS}" 2>/dev/null)
  1470→
  1471→  if [ -z "$PARSE_OUTPUT" ]; then
  1472→    echo "ERROR: Claude returned empty output while parsing requirements"
  1473→    exit 1
  1474→  fi
  1475→
  1476→  # Validate JSON
  1477→  if ! echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
  1478→    echo "ERROR: Claude returned invalid JSON:"
  1479→    echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT"
  1480→    exit 1
  1481→  fi
  1482→
  1483→  local STORY_COUNT
  1484→  STORY_COUNT=$(echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq length)
  1485→  log "Parsed $STORY_COUNT stories"
  1486→
  1487→  # Dry run — just print
  1488→  if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
  1489→    echo ""
  1490→    echo "=== Pipeline Dry Run ==="
  1491→    echo "Workspace: $WORKSPACE | Team: $TEAM_NAME"
  1492→    echo "Stories: $STORY_COUNT"
  1493→    echo ""
  1494→    echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.[] | "[\(.estimate)pt] \(.title)\n  Labels: \(.labels | join(", "))\n  Deps: \(.dependencies | if length > 0 then join(", ") else "none" end)\n  \(.description)\n"'
  1495→    return 0
  1496→  fi
  1497→
  1498→  # Get team ID
  1499→  local TEAM_ID
  1500→  TEAM_ID=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ teams(filter: { name: { eq: \"$TEAM_NAME\" } }) { nodes { id } } }" \
  1501→    | jq -r '.data.teams.nodes[0].id // empty')
  1502→
  1503→  if [ -z "$TEAM_ID" ]; then
  1504→    echo "ERROR: Team '$TEAM_NAME' not found in Linear"
  1505→    exit 1
  1506→  fi
  1507→
  1508→  # Get label ID for the agent label
  1509→  local AGENT_LABEL_ID
  1510→  AGENT_LABEL_ID=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "{ issueLabels(filter: { name: { eq: \"$LABEL\" } }) { nodes { id } } }" \
  1511→    | jq -r '.data.issueLabels.nodes[0].id // empty')
  1512→
  1513→  if [ -z "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" ]; then
  1514→    echo "WARNING: Label '$LABEL' not found in Linear — issues will be created without it"
  1515→  fi
  1516→
  1517→  # Create issues
  1518→  log "Creating $STORY_COUNT Linear issues..."
  1519→
  1520→  # Map title -> issue ID for dependency resolution
  1521→  declare -A TITLE_TO_ID
  1522→
  1523→  echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq -c '.[]' | while read -r story; do
  1524→    local STORY_TITLE STORY_DESC STORY_ESTIMATE
  1525→    STORY_TITLE=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.title')
  1526→    STORY_DESC=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.description')
  1527→    STORY_ESTIMATE=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.estimate // 0')
  1528→
  1529→    local LABEL_IDS=""
  1530→    if [ -n "$AGENT_LABEL_ID" ]; then
  1531→      # Check if this story has dependencies and --sequenced is set
  1532→      local HAS_DEPS
  1533→      HAS_DEPS=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.dependencies | length')
  1534→
  1535→      if [ "$SEQUENCED" = true ] && [ "$HAS_DEPS" -gt 0 ]; then
  1536→        # Don't add agent label — will be added when blockers complete
  1537→        LABEL_IDS="[]"
  1538→      else
  1539→        LABEL_IDS="[\"$AGENT_LABEL_ID\"]"
  1540→      fi
  1541→    else
  1542→      LABEL_IDS="[]"
  1543→    fi
  1544→
  1545→    local CREATE_RESULT
  1546→    CREATE_RESULT=$(linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation {
  1547→      issueCreate(input: {
  1548→        teamId: \"$TEAM_ID\"
  1549→        title: $(echo "$STORY_TITLE" | jq -Rs .)
  1550→        description: $(echo "$STORY_DESC" | jq -Rs .)
  1551→        estimate: $STORY_ESTIMATE
  1552→        labelIds: $LABEL_IDS
  1553→      }) {
  1554→        success
  1555→        issue { id identifier }
  1556→      }
  1557→    }")
  1558→
  1559→    local NEW_ID NEW_IDENT
  1560→    NEW_ID=$(echo "$CREATE_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.issueCreate.issue.id // empty')
  1561→    NEW_IDENT=$(echo "$CREATE_RESULT" | jq -r '.data.issueCreate.issue.identifier // empty')
  1562→
  1563→    if [ -n "$NEW_ID" ]; then
  1564→      log "Created: ${NEW_IDENT} — ${STORY_TITLE}"
  1565→      TITLE_TO_ID["$STORY_TITLE"]="$NEW_ID"
  1566→    else
  1567→      log "ERROR: Failed to create issue: ${STORY_TITLE}"
  1568→    fi
  1569→  done
  1570→
  1571→  # Create dependency relations (second pass)
  1572→  echo "$PARSE_OUTPUT" | jq -c '.[]' | while read -r story; do
  1573→    local STORY_TITLE
  1574→    STORY_TITLE=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.title')
  1575→    local DEPS
  1576→    DEPS=$(echo "$story" | jq -r '.dependencies[]?' 2>/dev/null)
  1577→
  1578→    [ -z "$DEPS" ] && continue
  1579→
  1580→    local THIS_ID="${TITLE_TO_ID[$STORY_TITLE]:-}"
  1581→    [ -z "$THIS_ID" ] && continue
  1582→
  1583→    while IFS= read -r dep_title; do
  1584→      local BLOCKER_ID="${TITLE_TO_ID[$dep_title]:-}"
  1585→      if [ -n "$BLOCKER_ID" ]; then
  1586→        linear_graphql "$API_KEY" "mutation {
  1587→          issueRelationCreate(input: {
  1588→            issueId: \"$THIS_ID\"
  1589→            relatedIssueId: \"$BLOCKER_ID\"
  1590→            type: \"isBlockedBy\"
  1591→          }) { success }
  1592→        }" >/dev/null 2>&1
  1593→        log "  Linked: ${STORY_TITLE} blocked by ${dep_title}"
  1594→      fi
  1595→    done <<< "$DEPS"
  1596→  done
  1597→
  1598→  log "Pipeline complete — $STORY_COUNT issues created in ${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}"
  1599→}
  1600→
  1601→# --- Spec command (Feature 2) ---
  1602→
  1603→run_spec() {
  1604→  local IDEA_FILE="$1"
  1605→  local WORKSPACE="${2:-}"
  1606→  local TEAM_NAME="${3:-}"
  1607→  local INTERACTIVE="${4:-false}"
  1608→  local CREATE="${5:-false}"
  1609→
  1610→  if [ ! -f "$IDEA_FILE" ]; then
  1611→    echo "ERROR: Idea file not found: $IDEA_FILE"
  1612→    exit 1
  1613→  fi
  1614→
  1615→  local IDEA
  1616→  IDEA=$(cat "$IDEA_FILE")
  1617→
  1618→  # Default to first workspace/team if not specified
  1619→  if [ -z "$WORKSPACE" ]; then
  1620→    WORKSPACE=$(jq -r '.workspaces | keys[0]' "$CONFIG")
  1621→  fi
  1622→  if [ -z "$TEAM_NAME" ]; then
  1623→    TEAM_NAME=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].teams | keys[0]" "$CONFIG")
  1624→  fi
  1625→
  1626→  log "Generating spec from idea..."
  1627→
  1628→  # Determine project repo for context
  1629→  local REPO_DIR
  1630→  REPO_DIR=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].teams[\"$TEAM_NAME\"].repo // empty" "$CONFIG")
  1631→
  1632→  local CONTEXT_PROMPT=""
  1633→  if [ -n "$REPO_DIR" ] && [ -d "$REPO_DIR" ]; then
  1634→    local CLAUDE_MD=""
  1635→    [ -f "$REPO_DIR/CLAUDE.md" ] && CLAUDE_MD=$(cat "$REPO_DIR/CLAUDE.md")
  1636→    if [ -n "$CLAUDE_MD" ]; then
  1637→      CONTEXT_PROMPT="
  1638→## Project Context (from CLAUDE.md)
  1639→${CLAUDE_MD}
  1640→"
  1641→    fi
  1642→  fi
  1643→
  1644→  local SPEC_PROMPT="Use the spec-writer skill to transform this idea into structured development requirements.
  1645→${CONTEXT_PROMPT}
  1646→## Idea
  1647→
  1648→${IDEA}
  1649→
  1650→## Output Format
  1651→
  1652→Output a markdown document compatible with the --pipeline command:
  1653→- Each story has a title, estimate, labels, dependencies, and description
  1654→- Stories should be sized for single agent sessions (1-3 hours)
  1655→- Include acceptance criteria for each story
  1656→- Note which stories should get the 'Orchestrate' label for multi-agent decomposition"
  1657→
  1658→  if [ "$INTERACTIVE" = true ]; then
  1659→    # Interactive mode — let Claude ask questions via stdin/stdout
  1660→    log "Running spec-writer in interactive mode..."
  1661→    if [ -n "$REPO_DIR" ] && [ -d "$REPO_DIR" ]; then
  1662→      cd "$REPO_DIR"
  1663→    fi
  1664→    claude --permission-mode plan --setting-sources project,local -p "$SPEC_PROMPT"
  1665→  else
  1666→    # Non-interactive — generate spec and output
  1667→    local SPEC_OUTPUT
  1668→    SPEC_OUTPUT=$(claude --print --permission-mode plan --setting-sources project,local -p "$SPEC_PROMPT" --max-turns 20 2>/dev/null)
  1669→
  1670→    if [ -z "$SPEC_OUTPUT" ]; then
  1671→      echo "ERROR: Claude returned empty output while generating spec"
  1672→      exit 1
  1673→    fi
  1674→
  1675→    echo "$SPEC_OUTPUT"
  1676→
  1677→    # If --create: save to temp file and chain to pipeline
  1678→    if [ "$CREATE" = true ]; then
  1679→      local SPEC_FILE
  1680→      SPEC_FILE=$(mktemp /tmp/spec-XXXXXX.md)
  1681→      echo "$SPEC_OUTPUT" > "$SPEC_FILE"
  1682→      log "Spec written to $SPEC_FILE — chaining to pipeline..."
  1683→      run_pipeline "$SPEC_FILE" "$WORKSPACE" "$TEAM_NAME" false
  1684→      rm -f "$SPEC_FILE"
  1685→    fi
  1686→  fi
  1687→}
  1688→
  1689→# --- Main agent loop ---
  1690→
  1691→run_agents_for_team() {
  1692→  local API_KEY="$1"
  1693→  local WORKSPACE="$2"
  1694→  local TEAM_NAME="$3"
  1695→  local REPO_DIR="$4"
  1696→  local LOCAL_DB="${5:-none}"
  1697→  local REPO_NAME
  1698→  REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")
  1699→  local SLACK_WEBHOOK
  1700→  SLACK_WEBHOOK=$(resolve_slack_webhook "$WORKSPACE")
  1701→
  1702→  # Lock per repo
  1703→  local LOCK_FILE="$LOCK_DIR/${REPO_NAME}.lock"
  1704→  if [ -f "$LOCK_FILE" ]; then
  1705→    local LOCK_PID
  1706→    LOCK_PID=$(cat "$LOCK_FILE")
  1707→    if kill -0 "$LOCK_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
  1708→      log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] Already running (PID $LOCK_PID), skipping"
  1709→      return 0
  1710→    else
  1711→      rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"
  1712→    fi
  1713→  fi
  1714→  echo $$ > "$LOCK_FILE"
  1715→
  1716→  cd "$REPO_DIR" || { log "[$WORKSPACE/$TEAM_NAME] Repo not found: $REPO_DIR"; rm -f "$LOCK_FILE"; return 1; }

   260→# --- GitHub PR feedback helpers ---
   261→
   262→gather_pr_feedback() {
   263→  local REPO_DIR="$1"
   264→  local BRANCH="$2"
   265→  local FEEDBACK=""
   266→
   267→  cd "$REPO_DIR" || return 0
   268→
   269→  # Check if a PR exists for this branch
   270→  local PR_NUMBER
   271→  PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json number --jq '.number' 2>/dev/null) || return 0
   272→  [ -z "$PR_NUMBER" ] && return 0
   273→
   274→  local OWNER_REPO
   275→  OWNER_REPO=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner --jq '.nameWithOwner' 2>/dev/null) || return 0
   276→
   277→  # Top-level PR comments
   278→  local PR_COMMENTS
   279→  PR_COMMENTS=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json comments --jq '.comments[] | "[\(.author.login)] \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null)
   280→  if [ -n "$PR_COMMENTS" ]; then
   281→    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## PR Comments
   282→${PR_COMMENTS}
   283→
   284→"
   285→  fi
   286→
   287→  # Review summaries
   288→  local REVIEWS
   289→  REVIEWS=$(gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json reviews --jq '.reviews[] | select(.body != "") | "[\(.author.login) - \(.state)] \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null)
   290→  if [ -n "$REVIEWS" ]; then
   291→    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## Review Summaries
   292→${REVIEWS}

290→  if [ -n "$REVIEWS" ]; then
   291→    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## Review Summaries
   292→${REVIEWS}
   293→
   294→"
   295→  fi
   296→
   297→  # Inline file-level comments (review comments on specific lines)
   298→  local INLINE
   299→  INLINE=$(gh api "repos/${OWNER_REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments" --jq '.[] | "[\(.user.login)] \(.path):\(.line // .original_line) — \(.body)"' 2>/dev/null)
   300→  if [ -n "$INLINE" ]; then
   301→    FEEDBACK="${FEEDBACK}## Inline Review Comments
   302→${INLINE}
   303→
   304→"
   305→  fi
   306→
   307→  echo "$FEEDBACK"
   308→}

   312→slack_notify() {

   315→  [ -z "$WEBHOOK_URL" ] && return 0
   316→  curl -s -X POST "$WEBHOOK_URL" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \

   320→# --- Resolve API key for a workspace ---
   321→
   322→resolve_api_key() {
   323→  local WORKSPACE="$1"
   324→
   325→  # Check for env var name in config
   326→  local ENV_VAR
   327→  ENV_VAR=$(jq -r ".workspaces[\"$WORKSPACE\"].api_key_env // empty" "$CONFIG")
   328→  if [ -n "$ENV_VAR" ]; then
   329→    local KEY="${!ENV_VAR:-}"
   330→    if [ -n "$KEY" ]; then
   331→      echo "$KEY"
   332→      return 0
   333→    fi
   334→    echo "WARNING: env var $ENV_VAR not set for workspace $WORKSPACE" >&2
   335→  fi
   336→
   337→  # Fallback: LINEAR_API_KEY_<WORKSPACE>
   338→  local AUTO_VAR="LINEAR_API_KEY_$(echo "$WORKSPACE" | tr '[:lower:]-' '[:upper:]_')"
   339→  local KEY="${!AUTO_VAR:-}"
   340→  if [ -n "$KEY" ]; then
   341→    echo "$KEY"
   342→    return 0
   343→  fi
   344→
   345→  # Last fallback: global LINEAR_API_KEY
   346→  if [ -n "${LINEAR_API_KEY:-}" ]; then
   347→    echo "$LINEAR_API_KEY"
   348→    return 0
   349→  fi
   350→
   351→  echo "ERROR: No API key found for workspace $WORKSPACE" >&2
   352→  echo "  Set one of: $ENV_VAR, $AUTO_VAR, or LINEAR_API_KEY" >&2
   353→  return 1
   354→}

   367→      return 0
   368→    fi
   369→  fi

   372→  local URL="${!AUTO_VAR:-}"
   373→  if [ -n "$URL" ]; then
   374→    echo "$URL"
   375→    return 0
   376→  fi

   381→# --- Docker-per-agent DB isolation ---
   382→
   383→start_agent_db() {
   384→  local WORKTREE="$1"
   385→  local IDENTIFIER="$2"
   386→  local REPO_DIR="$3"
   387→  local DB_TYPE="${4:-none}"
   388→
   389→  [ "$DB_TYPE" = "none" ] && return 0
   390→
   391→  local REPO_NAME
   392→  REPO_NAME=$(basename "$REPO_DIR")
   393→  local CONTAINER="agent-db-${IDENTIFIER}"
   394→
   395→  # On resume: check if container is already running
   396→  if docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -qx "$CONTAINER"; then
   397→    log "[$REPO_NAME] DB container $CONTAINER already running, reusing"
   398→    local EXISTING_PORT
   399→    EXISTING_PORT=$(docker port "$CONTAINER" 5432 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d: -f2)
   400→    if [ -n "$EXISTING_PORT" ]; then
   401→      _write_db_env "$WORKTREE" "$EXISTING_PORT" "$DB_TYPE"
   402→    fi
   403→    return 0
   404→  fi
   405→
   406→  # Remove stopped container with same name if it exists
   407→  docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   408→
   409→  log "[$REPO_NAME] Starting DB container for ${IDENTIFIER} (type: ${DB_TYPE})"
   410→
   411→  # Start postgres container with random ephemeral port
   412→  docker run -d \
   413→    --name "$CONTAINER" \
   414→    --label "claude-agent=true" \
   415→    -e POSTGRES_USER=postgres \
   416→    -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \
   417→    -e POSTGRES_DB=agent \
   418→    -p 0:5432 \
   419→    postgres:16-alpine >/dev/null 2>&1
   420→
   421→  if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
   422→    log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: Failed to start DB container for ${IDENTIFIER}"
   423→    return 1
   424→  fi
   425→
   426→  # Read assigned port
   427→  local DB_PORT
   428→  DB_PORT=$(docker port "$CONTAINER" 5432 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d: -f2)
   429→  if [ -z "$DB_PORT" ]; then
   430→    log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: Could not determine DB port for ${IDENTIFIER}"
   431→    docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1
   432→    return 1
   433→  fi
   434→
   435→  # Wait for pg_isready (max 30s)
   436→  local WAITED=0
   437→  while [ $WAITED -lt 30 ]; do
   438→    if docker exec "$CONTAINER" pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   439→      break

439→      break
   440→    fi
   441→    sleep 1
   442→    WAITED=$((WAITED + 1))
   443→  done
   444→
   445→  if [ $WAITED -ge 30 ]; then
   446→    log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: DB container for ${IDENTIFIER} not ready after 30s"
   447→    docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1
   448→    return 1
   449→  fi
   450→
   451→  # Run migrations if present
   452→  local MIGRATION_DIR=""
   453→  if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/supabase/migrations" ]; then
   454→    MIGRATION_DIR="$REPO_DIR/supabase/migrations"
   455→  elif [ -d "$REPO_DIR/migrations" ]; then
   456→    MIGRATION_DIR="$REPO_DIR/migrations"
   457→  fi
   458→
   459→  if [ -n "$MIGRATION_DIR" ]; then
   460→    log "[$REPO_NAME] Running migrations from $MIGRATION_DIR"
   461→    for sql_file in "$MIGRATION_DIR"/*.sql; do
   462→      [ -f "$sql_file" ] || continue
   463→      docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d agent < "$sql_file" >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
   464→        log "[$REPO_NAME] WARNING: Migration failed: $(basename "$sql_file")"
   465→      }
   466→    done
   467→  fi
   468→
   469→  _write_db_env "$WORKTREE" "$DB_PORT" "$DB_TYPE"
   470→  log "[$REPO_NAME] DB running for ${IDENTIFIER} on port ${DB_PORT}"
   471→  trace_event "db.started" "type=$DB_TYPE" "port=$DB_PORT"
   472→  return 0
   473→}
   474→
   475→_write_db_env() {
   476→  local WORKTREE="$1"
   477→  local DB_PORT="$2"
   478→  local DB_TYPE="$3"
   479→
   480→  local DB_URL="postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:${DB_PORT}/agent"
   481→
   482→  # Strip existing DB env vars if .env.local exists
   483→  if [ -f "$WORKTREE/.env.local" ]; then
   484→    grep -v '^DATABASE_URL=' "$WORKTREE/.env.local" \
   485→      | grep -v '^DIRECT_URL=' \
   486→      | grep -v '^NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=' \
   487→      | grep -v '^SUPABASE_DB_URL=' \
   488→      > "$WORKTREE/.env.local.tmp" || true
   489→    mv "$WORKTREE/.env.local.tmp" "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
   490→  fi
   491→
   492→  echo "DATABASE_URL=${DB_URL}" >> "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
   493→  echo "DIRECT_URL=${DB_URL}" >> "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
   494→
   495→  if [ "$DB_TYPE" = "supabase" ]; then
   496→    echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=http://localhost:${DB_PORT}" >> "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
   497→    echo "SUPABASE_DB_URL=${DB_URL}" >> "$WORKTREE/.env.local"
   498→  fi
   499→}
   500→
   501→stop_agent_db() {
   502→  local IDENTIFIER="$1"
   503→  local CONTAINER="agent-db-${IDENTIFIER}"
   504→  docker rm -f "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
   505→  trace_event "db.stopped"
   506→}

Perfect! Now let me compile my comprehensive findings into a structured report:

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: claude-agent-runner Lifecycle

I've thoroughly explored the codebase. Here's the complete breakdown of all 12 flows you requested:

---

### **1. MAIN LOOP / POLLING LOGIC**

**Function: `run_agents_for_team()` (lines 1691-2322)**

The main polling loop:
1. **Lock management** (lines 1703-1714): Checks per-repo lock file to prevent parallel runs; uses process PID
2. **Cleanup** (line 1718): Calls `cleanup_worktrees()` to remove merged PRs
3. **Slot counting** (lines 1720-1746): Tracks active agents by reading `.claude/agent.pid` files in worktrees; calculates available slots based on `MAX_PARALLEL` config
4. **Issue fetching** (lines 1750-1758): Calls `fetch_agent_issues()` with the API key, team name, and available slots
5. **Per-issue loop** (lines 1770-2319): For each issue:
   - Extracts ID, identifier, title, description
   - Generates branch name and worktree path
   - Detects resume vs. new issue (lines 1795-1819)
   - Spawns agent subprocess in background (line 2314) with PID tracking

**Helper: `fetch_agent_issues()` (lines 522-595)**
- Uses Linear GraphQL query to find issues with configurable label (default: "agent")
- Queries: id, identifier, title, description, team, parent, labels, state
- **With --sequenced flag**: Also fetches relations to filter out issues with incomplete blockers (lines 586-591)
- Returns array of issues up to `LIMIT` (available slots)

**Entry point for polling (line 2337-2346)**:
```bash
case "${1:-}" in
  --all|"") 
    # Default: iterate all workspaces/teams from config.json
    # Calls run_agents_for_team for each
```

---

### **2. WORKTREE CREATION FLOW**

**Location: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 1809-1819**

**Full flow:**
1. **Detect resume** (lines 1795-1806):
   - If worktree already exists, check for `.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md` → resume reason = "blocked"
   - Otherwise → resume reason = "feedback" (PR feedback resume)
   - Trace event: `worktree.reused`

2. **Create new worktree** (lines 1809-1819):
   ```bash
   git fetch origin main
   git worktree add "$WORKTREE" -b "$BRANCH" origin/main
   ```
   - Worktree path: `~/.claude/worktrees/${REPO_NAME}/issue-${IDENTIFIER}/`
   - Branch: `agent/${IDENTIFIER}-${TITLE_SLUG}`
   - Copy `.env` and `.env.local` from repo root (lines 1816-1817)
   - Trace event: `worktree.created`

3. **Update Linear state** (lines 1822-1823):
   - Call `linear_set_state()` → "In Progress"
   - Call `linear_remove_label()` → Remove the "agent" label

4. **Start DB container if configured** (lines 1826-1828):
   - Calls `start_agent_db()` (see flow #5)

5. **Spawn agent subprocess** (lines 1832-2316):
   - Subshell: `(...) >> "$ISSUE_LOG" 2>&1 &`
   - Records PID to `$WORKTREE/.claude/agent.pid`

---

### **3. CLAUDE INVOCATION (SPAWNING)**

**Location: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 1832-2049 (standard), 1879-1941 (resume), 1947-2052 (orchestrator/scoped)**

**Environment exports:**
```bash
export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1              # Enables guardrail hooks
export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO="$REPO_NAME"
export CLAUDE_AGENT_BRANCH="$BRANCH"
export CLAUDE_AGENT_ISSUE_ID="$IDENTIFIER"
export CLAUDE_AGENT_WORKTREE="$WORKTREE"
unset CLAUDECODE                         # Allow spawning Claude from within Claude
```

**Standard single-agent invocation** (lines 1978-2049):
```bash
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
  --setting-sources project,local \
  -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for all project conventions...
## Linear Issue: ${IDENTIFIER}
**Title:** ${TITLE}
**Description:** ${DESCRIPTION}
..." \
  --max-turns 500
```

**Resume: Blocked question** (lines 1889-1896):
```bash
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
  --setting-sources project,local \
  -r "$PREV_SESSION" \  # Resume from saved session ID
  -p "The developer has responded to your question. Here are the latest comments from Linear:
${COMMENTS}
Continue implementing ${IDENTIFIER}..." \
  --max-turns 500
```

**Resume: Feedback on PR** (lines 1936-1939):
```bash
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
  --setting-sources project,local \
  [-r "$PREV_SESSION"] \
  -p "## Feedback Resume: ${IDENTIFIER} — ${TITLE}
Reviewers have left feedback on your PR and/or the Linear ticket. Address ALL feedback below..."
```

**Session ID persistence** (lines 1876-1882, 2055-2063):
- Saves session ID to `$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-session.id`
- Reads from `~/.claude/projects/<worktree-escaped>/` latest .jsonl file
- Used for resuming across feedback cycles

---

### **4. CI GATE / RETRY LOOP**

**Location: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 2084-2193**

**Flow:**
1. **Check commits** (lines 2085-2096):
   - Count: `git rev-list --count origin/main..HEAD`
   - If 0 commits → exit with warning; no push/PR created

2. **Check blocking file** (lines 2066-2082):
   - If `.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md` exists → post to Linear, set state to "Todo", exit
   - Trace event: `agent.blocked`

3. **CI gate retry loop** (lines 2102-2193):
   - **Attempt count**: 1 to `MAX_CI_RETRIES` (default 3)
   - **Each attempt**:
     - Call `$CI_GATE "$WORKTREE"` (see `/bin/ci-gate` at the end)
     - Log output to `$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-ci-${ATTEMPT}.log`
     - Trace event: `ci.passed` or `ci.failed`
     - Post status comment to Linear (lines 2104)
   
   - **If CI fails** (lines 2113-2142):
     - First 2 attempts (< MAX_CI_RETRIES):
       - Extract error output
       - Spawn Claude fix agent (lines 2123-2142):
         ```bash
         claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
           --setting-sources project,local \
           -p "## CI Gate Failed — Fix These Errors
         ${CI_ERRORS}
         ..." --max-turns 500
         ```
       - Trace event: `ci_fix.started` / `ci_fix.finished`
       - Loop continues
     
     - Final failure (attempt == MAX_CI_RETRIES):
       - Build failure report from all CI logs (lines 2159-2185)
       - Post to Linear as comment with report
       - Post to Slack
       - Trace event: none (logged as exit)
       - Exit early

4. **If CI passes** (lines 2195-2277):
   - Clean up transient status comments
   - Post agent report to Linear if exists (lines 2202-2204)
   - Push branch: `git push -u origin "$BRANCH"`
   - Trace event: `pr.pushed`

**ci-gate tool** (`/bin/ci-gate` lines 1-100+):
- Detects project type: TypeScript/Next.js, Rust, Python, Go
- Runs: `tsc --noEmit`, tests (vitest/jest/pytest), build (next/vite/cargo/go)
- Can use project-specific `.ci-gate` file with custom commands
- Exit 0 = pass, Exit 1 = fail

---

### **5. PR CREATION & POSTING FLOW**

**Location: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 2211-2275**

**Two paths: feedback update vs. new PR**

**Path A: Feedback-addressed PR (lines 2213-2228)**:
- Check if existing PR: `gh pr view "$BRANCH"`
- If exists:
  - Set Linear state to "In Review"
  - Post comment: "✅ **Feedback addressed** — new commits pushed to existing PR: ${EXISTING_PR}"
  - Post to Slack
  - If `AUTO_REVIEW=true`: Call `auto_review_pr()` (see flow #6)
  - If `AUTO_FIX=true` AND verdict is "request_changes": Call `auto_fix_loop()` (see flow #6)

**Path B: New PR creation** (lines 2229-2275):
1. **Build PR body** (lines 2230-2245):
   - Check for `.claude/agent-reports/${IDENTIFIER}.md` (implementation report)
   - If exists: use as PR body
   - If not: use issue title + description
   - Append footer: "*Implemented by autonomous Claude agent. CI gate passed: type-check, tests, build.*"
   - Close with `Closes ${IDENTIFIER}`

2. **Create draft PR** (lines 2247-2252):
   ```bash
   gh pr create \
     --draft \
     --title "${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}" \
     --body "$PR_BODY" \
     --head "$BRANCH" \
     --base main
   ```
   - Trace event: `pr.created url=$PR_URL`

3. **Update Linear & Slack** (lines 2254-2260):
   - Set state: "In Review"
   - Post comment: "✅ **Agent completed** — CI gate passed, PR opened: ${PR_URL}"
   - Post to Slack
   - Log to issue log

4. **Auto-review (if enabled)** (lines 2264-2270):
   - Same as feedback path

---

### **6. AUTO-REVIEW & AUTO-FIX CYCLES**

**Auto-review: `auto_review_pr()` (lines 679-798)**

**Flow:**
1. **Fetch PR info**:
   - Get PR number from branch
   - Get full diff: `gh pr diff "$BRANCH"`
   - Get title and body

2. **Spawn review agent** (line 739):
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode plan --print \
     -p "You are a senior code reviewer. Review this PR thoroughly.
   [Full PR title, description, and diff]
   
   Output ONLY a JSON object with this exact structure:
   {
     \"verdict\": \"approve\" | \"request_changes\" | \"comment\",
     \"summary\": \"...\",
     \"blocking\": [...],
     \"important\": [...],
     \"nits\": [...],
     \"praise\": [...]
   }" --max-turns 20
   ```
   - Mode: `--permission-mode plan` (read-only, safe)
   - Expected output: JSON with structured review

3. **Parse review** (lines 749-794):
   - Extract verdict, summary, blocking/important/nits/praise
   - If JSON valid:
     - Format as markdown
     - Post via `gh pr review` with verdict (approve/request-changes/comment)
   - If non-JSON:
     - Post as plain comment
   - Trace event: `review.completed verdict=$VERDICT pr_number=$PR_NUMBER`

4. **Return verdict** (line 797):
   - Echo "approve" | "request_changes" | "comment"

**Auto-fix loop: `auto_fix_loop()` (lines 802-904)**

**Triggered if:** `AUTO_REVIEW=true` AND review verdict = "request_changes"

**Iteration loop** (lines 814-900, up to `MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS` — default 3):

1. **Check latest review state** (lines 820-830):
   - `gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json reviews`
   - If latest state == "APPROVED" → exit loop
   - If latest state != "CHANGES_REQUESTED" → exit loop

2. **Gather feedback** (lines 833-839):
   - Call `gather_pr_feedback()` (see below)
   - If empty → exit loop

3. **Spawn fix agent** (lines 842-862):
   ```bash
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO / BRANCH / ISSUE_ID / WORKTREE
   
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
   
   ## Fix PR Feedback: ${IDENTIFIER}
   
   Address ALL reviewer feedback below. Make new, atomic commits for each fix.
   ${PR_FEEDBACK}
   ..." --max-turns 500
   ```
   - Trace event: `fix.started iteration=$ITER`

4. **Run CI gate on fixes** (lines 867-882):
   - Same retry logic as standard flow
   - If fails: spawn one inline Claude retry (lines 871-875)
   - If still fails: exit loop
   - Trace event: `fix.ci_failed iteration=$ITER` → return 1

5. **Push fixes & re-review** (lines 886-900):
   - `git push origin "$BRANCH"`
   - Call `auto_review_pr()` again
   - If verdict == "approve" → exit loop successfully (trace: `fix.completed`)
   - Otherwise: continue to next iteration

6. **Loop exhaustion** (lines 901-903):
   - Log: "Exhausted ${MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS} fix iterations"
   - Return 1

**Helper: `gather_pr_feedback()` (lines 262-308)**

Collects all feedback:
1. **PR top-level comments**: `gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json comments`
2. **Review body comments**: `gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json reviews`
3. **Inline file comments**: `gh api repos/${OWNER_REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments`

Returns markdown-formatted feedback with author, file:line, and comment body.

---

### **7. ORCHESTRATOR FLOW**

**Main entry: `orchestrate_issue()` (lines 931-1357)**

**Triggered by:**
- `ORCH_ENABLED=true` in config
- Issue has `Orchestrate` label (checked by `should_orchestrate()` at line 909-918)
- Called from `run_agents_for_team()` at line 1952

**5-phase orchestration process:**

**PHASE 1: Decomposition (lines 955-983)**
1. Create `.claude/orchestrator/` directory in worktree
2. Export `CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1` and agent env vars
3. Spawn orchestrator Claude (lines 962-983):
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     -p "You are an orchestrator decomposing a Linear issue into subtasks.
   
   ## Parent Issue: ${IDENTIFIER}
   **Title:** ${TITLE}
   **Description:** ${DESCRIPTION}
   
   ## Instructions
   Use the orchestrate skill to decompose this issue into scoped subtasks.
   Write subtask JSON files to .claude/orchestrator/subtask-NNN.json
   
   If this issue is single-concern and doesn't need decomposition, write:
   {\"no_decomposition\": true, \"reason\": \"...\"}
   to .claude/orchestrator/no_decomposition.json
   
   Max subtasks: ${ORCH_MAX_SUBTASKS}
   ..." --max-turns "$ORCH_MAX_TURNS"
   ```
   - Trace event: `orchestrator.fallback reason=no_decomposition` or `orchestrator.decomposed count=$SUBTASK_COUNT`

4. **Check fallback conditions** (lines 985-1011):
   - If `no_decomposition.json` exists → return 1 (fall back to single agent)
   - If subtask count < 2 → return 1

**PHASE 2: Create sub-issues in Linear (lines 1024-1084)**

For each `subtask-NNN.json` file:
1. Extract: id, title, skill, scope, off_limits, shared_files, acceptance_criteria, context, depends_on
2. Build sub-issue description with scope metadata (lines 1038-1058):
   ```
   ## Subtask of ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}
   ${ST_CONTEXT}
   
   ## File Scope
   **Allowed (write):** ${ST_SCOPE}
   **Off limits:** ...
   **Shared files (read-only):** ...
   
   ## Acceptance Criteria
   ${ST_CRITERIA}
   
   ## Dependencies
   ${ST_DEPS}
   ```

3. Create via `linear_create_sub_issue()` (lines 1062-1063):
   - Mutation: issueCreate with parentId=ISSUE_ID, add agent label
   - Track in STATE_SUBTASKS array (lines 1074-1079)
   - Trace event: `subtask.created subtask_id=$ST_ID identifier=$SUB_IDENTIFIER`

4. **Create dependency relations** (lines 1085-1116):
   - For each subtask with `depends_on[]`:
     - Remove agent label (won't get picked until unblocked)
     - Call `issueRelationCreate` with type="isBlockedBy"

5. **Write state.json** (lines 1122-1136):
   ```json
   {
     "parent_id": "${IDENTIFIER}",
     "branch": "agent/${IDENTIFIER}",
     "issue_id": "${ISSUE_ID}",
     "subtasks": [{"id": "ST-1", "linear_id": "...", "status": "pending", "skill": "..."}],
     "shared_files": [...],
     "created_at": "...",
     "status": "active"
   }
   ```

6. **Commit and notify** (lines 1155-1153):
   - `git add .claude/orchestrator/ && git commit`
   - Post to Linear: subtask summary with links
   - Trace event: orchestrator state saved

**PHASE 3: Supervisor loop (lines 1161-1234)**

Polls every `ORCH_POLL_INTERVAL` seconds (default 30) until all subtasks done:

1. Read state.json
2. For each non-completed subtask:
   - Query Linear: `linear_get_issue_state()` → type (completed/canceled/pending/todo/etc)
   - If type == "completed":
     - Check for scope-overflow file: `.claude/orchestrator/scope-overflow-${ST_ID}.md`
     - If overflow exists: post to Linear about follow-up
     - Update STATE_SUBTASKS status → "completed"
     - Trace event: `subtask.completed subtask_id=$ST_ID`
   - If type == "canceled":
     - Update status → "canceled"
   - Otherwise: ALL_DONE=false

3. Update state.json with new subtask statuses
4. If ALL_DONE → break loop

**PHASE 4: Integration validation (lines 1239-1290)**

1. Pull latest changes from subtask workers:
   - `git pull origin "agent/${IDENTIFIER}"`

2. Run CI gate on integrated result (lines 1244-1290):
   - If passes → INTEGRATION_PASSED=true, trace: `integration.passed`
   - If fails:
     - Extract error output
     - Spawn fix agent (lines 1262-1276):
       ```bash
       claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
         --setting-sources project,local \
         -p "This is an orchestrated feature (${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}) with multiple subtasks...
       ## Integration CI Failed — Fix These Errors
       [errors]
       ..." --max-turns 500
       ```
     - Retry CI (lines 1279-1286)
     - Trace: `integration.failed` or `integration.passed after_fix=true`

**PHASE 5: Create combined PR (lines 1292-1356)**

1. Push: `git push -u origin "agent/${IDENTIFIER}"`

2. Build PR body from all subtask reports (lines 1299-1320):
   ```
   ## ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}
   
   ### Orchestrated Implementation
   This feature was decomposed into ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks...
   
   ### Subtask Reports
   [each subtask's .claude/agent-reports/${ST_IDENT}.md]
   
   Closes ${IDENTIFIER}
   ```

3. Create PR via `gh pr create` (lines 1327-1333)

4. **Success path** (lines 1335-1343):
   - Set Linear state to "In Review"
   - Post: "✅ **Orchestrator completed** — all ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks done, CI passed, PR: ${PR_URL}"
   - Update state.json status → "completed"
   - Return 0

5. **Failure path** (lines 1344-1349):
   - Post: "❌ **Orchestrator integration CI failed** — needs human intervention"
   - Return 0 (don't crash main flow)

**Scoped worker: `run_scoped_worker()` (lines 1360-1413)**

Called when issue has parent (orchestrated subtask):

1. **Extract scope from description** (lines 1367-1380):
   - Parse "## File Scope" section for "Allowed (write):" line
   - Extract comma-separated paths

2. **Create scope file** (lines 1375-1383):
   - Path: `.claude/orchestrator/active-scope.json`
   - Format: `{"scope": ["path/a", "path/b"]}`

3. **Set scoped environment** (lines 1386-1391):
   ```bash
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPED=1
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPE_FILE="$SCOPE_FILE"
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO / BRANCH / ISSUE_ID / WORKTREE
   ```

4. **Spawn scoped worker Claude** (lines 1395-1409):
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     -p "You are a scoped worker in an isolated git worktree...
   
   ## Your Subtask: ${IDENTIFIER} — ${TITLE}
   
   Use the scoped-worker skill for your implementation workflow.
   
   [Full subtask description with scope/criteria/deps]
   
   ## IMPORTANT
   - Your file writes are enforced by the scope-guard hook
   - If you need out-of-scope changes, write to .claude/orchestrator/scope-overflow
   ..." --max-turns 500
   ```
   - Trace event: `agent.started mode=scoped-worker`

---

### **8. FEEDBACK/RESUME FLOW**

**Entry: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 1793-1942**

**Automatic detection:**
- If worktree already exists: RESUMING=true
- Check for `.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md`:
  - Yes → RESUME_REASON="blocked"
  - No → RESUME_REASON="feedback"

**Resume blocked** (lines 1882-1900):
1. Read new comments from Linear: `linear_get_comments()`
2. Post: "🔄 **Agent resuming** — developer responded, continuing work"
3. Spawn Claude with -r flag (resume session):
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     -r "$PREV_SESSION" \
     -p "The developer has responded to your question. Here are the latest comments:
   ${COMMENTS}
   
   Continue implementing ${IDENTIFIER}..."
   ```
4. Trace: `issue.resumed reason=blocked`

**Resume feedback** (lines 1902-1941):
1. Gather PR and Linear feedback: `gather_pr_feedback()` + `linear_get_comments()`
2. Post: "🔄 **Agent resuming** — addressing reviewer feedback"
3. Build feedback prompt with all comments
4. Spawn Claude:
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     [-r "$PREV_SESSION"] \
     -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree...
   
   ## Feedback Resume: ${IDENTIFIER} — ${TITLE}
   
   Reviewers have left feedback on your PR and/or the Linear ticket. Address ALL feedback below with new commits.
   
   [PR Feedback section]
   [Linear Ticket Comments section]
   
   Address every piece of feedback. If unclear, write to .claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md"
   ```
5. Trace: `issue.resumed reason=feedback`

**Persistence mechanism** (lines 2055-2063):
- After Claude finishes, extract session ID from `~/.claude/projects/<worktree-escaped>/<latest>.jsonl`
- Save to `$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-session.id`
- On next resume, read session ID and use with `-r` flag

**Triggering resume:**
- Developer re-adds the "agent" label to the issue in Linear
- Next `run_agents_for_team()` poll picks it up
- System detects existing worktree and resumes

---

### **9. --SPEC FLOW END-TO-END**

**Entry: `run_spec()` (lines 1603-1687)**

**Command invocation:**
```bash
claude-agent-runner --spec <idea.md> [--workspace <name>] [--team <name>] [--interactive] [--create]
```

**Flow:**

1. **Load idea file** (line 1616):
   - Read markdown file with freeform idea

2. **Resolve workspace/team** (lines 1619-1624):
   - Default to first in config if not specified

3. **Load project context** (lines 1632-1642):
   - Read project CLAUDE.md if repo available
   - Include in spec prompt for context-aware generation

4. **Choose mode** (lines 1658-1686):

   **Interactive mode** (`--interactive` flag):
   - Spawn Claude in interactive mode:
     ```bash
     claude --permission-mode plan \
       --setting-sources project,local \
       -p "Use the spec-writer skill to transform this idea into structured development requirements.
     
     ## Project Context (from CLAUDE.md)
     ${CLAUDE_MD}
     
     ## Idea
     ${IDEA}
     
     ## Output Format
     Output a markdown document compatible with the --pipeline command:
     - Each story has a title, estimate, labels, dependencies, and description
     - Stories should be sized for single agent sessions (1-3 hours)
     - Include acceptance criteria for each story
     - Note which stories should get the 'Orchestrate' label"
     ```
   - User can ask questions interactively

   **Non-interactive mode** (default):
   - Spawn Claude with --max-turns 20:
     ```bash
     claude --print --permission-mode plan \
       --setting-sources project,local \
       -p "[same prompt as above]" \
       --max-turns 20
     ```
   - Claude generates spec in one shot
   - Output printed to stdout

5. **Chain to pipeline** (`--create` flag):
   - Save spec output to temp file
   - Call `run_pipeline()` (see flow #10) with that file
   - Clean up temp file

**Output format:**
- Markdown compatible with `--pipeline`
- Each story:
  ```
  - **Title**: Short imperative
  - **Estimate**: 1-8 story points
  - **Labels**: feature/bug/chore/improvement
  - **Dependencies**: [list of other story titles]
  - **Description**: Detailed description with acceptance criteria
  ```

---

### **10. --PIPELINE FLOW END-TO-END**

**Entry: `run_pipeline()` (lines 1417-1599)**

**Command invocation:**
```bash
claude-agent-runner --pipeline <requirements.md> [--workspace <name>] [--team <name>] [--dry-run]
```

**Flow:**

1. **Load requirements file** (lines 1428-1429):
   - Read markdown containing structured requirements

2. **Resolve workspace/team** (lines 1431-1437):
   - Default to first in config if not specified

3. **Spawn parser Claude** (lines 1445-1469):
   ```bash
   claude --print --max-turns 3 --output-format json \
     -p "Parse the following product requirements into structured development stories.
   
   Output ONLY a JSON array with this structure:
   [
     {
       \"title\": \"Short imperative title\",
       \"description\": \"Detailed description with acceptance criteria\",
       \"estimate\": 1,
       \"labels\": [\"feature\"],
       \"dependencies\": []
     }
   ]
   
   Rules:
   - estimate is in story points (1-8)
   - labels: use 'feature', 'bug', 'chore', 'improvement'
   - dependencies: list titles of other stories this depends on
   - Break large requirements into small, independent stories
   - Each story completable in 1-3 hours
   - Output ONLY the JSON array, nothing else
   
   [requirements]"
   ```

4. **Validate JSON** (lines 1476-1481):
   - Parse output with jq
   - Exit if invalid

5. **Dry-run mode** (`--dry-run`):
   - Pretty-print stories with estimate, labels, dependencies, description
   - Exit (no Linear issue creation)

6. **Get team/label IDs** (lines 1498-1515):
   - Query Linear for team ID (by name)
   - Query Linear for agent label ID (by name)
   - Warn if label not found

7. **Create issues** (lines 1523-1569):
   - For each story in parsed JSON:
     - Create via `linear_graphql()` issueCreate mutation
     - Set: teamId, title, description, estimate, labelIds
     - **Conditional labeling** (lines 1531-1543):
       - If `--sequenced` flag AND story has dependencies:
         - Don't add agent label (wait for blocker completion)
       - Else:
         - Add agent label (ready for agent to pick up)
     - Track title → issue ID mapping (TITLE_TO_ID)
     - Trace event: issues created

8. **Create dependency relations** (lines 1571-1596):
   - Second pass through stories
   - For each story with dependencies:
     - Look up dependency story IDs via TITLE_TO_ID
     - Call `issueRelationCreate()` mutation with type="isBlockedBy"
     - Log "Linked: story_title blocked by dependency_title"

9. **Complete** (line 1598):
   - Log: "Pipeline complete — $STORY_COUNT issues created in ${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}"

---

### **11. SLACK NOTIFICATION FLOW**

**Function: `slack_notify()` (lines 312-318)**

```bash
slack_notify() {
  local WEBHOOK_URL="$1"
  local MESSAGE="$2"
  [ -z "$WEBHOOK_URL" ] && return 0
  curl -s -X POST "$WEBHOOK_URL" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d "{\"text\": $(echo "$MESSAGE" | jq -Rs .)}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}
```

**Integration points:**

1. **Resolver: `resolve_slack_webhook()` (lines 358-379)**
   - Check config for `workspaces[WORKSPACE].slack_webhook_env` env var name
   - Fallback: `SLACK_WEBHOOK_${WORKSPACE}` (uppercase)
   - Return empty string if not configured (silent no-op)

2. **Notification triggers** (all in `run_agents_for_team()`):

   | Event | Message | Line |
   |-------|---------|------|
   | Resume (blocked) | 🔄 Agent resuming — developer responded to blocked question | 1886 |
   | Resume (feedback) | 🔄 Agent resuming — addressing PR/Linear feedback | 1907 |
   | Orchestrator start | 🧩 Orchestrator starting for ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE} | 1950 |
   | Scoped worker start | 🔧 Scoped worker started ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE} | 1968 |
   | Standard agent start | 🔧 Agent started ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE} | 1976 |
   | Agent blocked | 🚧 Agent blocked on ${IDENTIFIER} — needs human input | 2078 |
   | Feedback addressed | ✅ ${IDENTIFIER} feedback addressed — PR updated: ${EXISTING_PR} | 2218 |
   | PR created | ✅ ${IDENTIFIER} completed — PR opened: ${PR_URL} | 2260 |
   | PR creation failed | ❌ Push succeeded but PR creation failed | (N/A in notification) |
   | CI passed (no gate) | ✅ ${IDENTIFIER} completed (no CI gate) — PR: ${PR_URL} | 2298 |
   | Orchestrator completed | ✅ Orchestrated ${IDENTIFIER} completed — ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks, PR: ${PR_URL} | 1338 |
   | Orchestrator CI failed | ❌ Orchestrated ${IDENTIFIER} integration CI failed — needs human intervention | 1348 |

---

### **12. BLOCKING FLOW**

**Entry: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 2065-2082**

**Blocking file detection and handling:**

1. **Check for block file** (line 2066):
   - Path: `.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md`
   - Written by agent during implementation if blocked on questions

2. **If file exists** (lines 2067-2081):
   - Read content: `QUESTION=$(cat "$BLOCKED_FILE")`
   - Trace event: `agent.blocked`
   - Clean up transient status comments
   - Post to Linear (lines 2071-2076):
     ```
     🚧 **Agent blocked — needs human input**
     
     ${QUESTION}
     
     ---
     *Reply to this comment, then re-add the `${LABEL}` label to resume the agent.*
     ```
   - Set issue state to "Todo"
   - Post to Slack
   - Log message
   - Stop DB container
   - **Exit immediately** (don't push/open PR)

**How blocking is triggered** (agent side):

In Claude prompt (lines 1992-2002):
```
## CRITICAL: If You Are Blocked or Need Clarification

YOU MUST:
1. Create the file `.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md` with your question(s)
2. `git add` and `git commit` it
3. STOP immediately — do NOT continue working, do NOT try to guess

The ONLY way to ask the developer a question is through the blocked file.
If you have PARTIAL work done but still need answers, commit your partial work
AND the blocked file together.
```

**Resume after blocking:**

1. Developer sees blocking message in Linear
2. Developer replies in Linear comments
3. Developer re-adds the "agent" label to the issue
4. Next `run_agents_for_team()` poll detects existing worktree + absence of blocked file
5. Sets RESUME_REASON="blocked"
6. Spawns Claude with `-r` flag to resume previous session
7. Passes latest Linear comments as context

---

### **13. SUPPORTING COMMANDS**

**`--status` flag** → `show_status()` (lines 635-675)
- Lists all workspaces/teams
- Shows active agents (reading .claude/agent.pid files)
- Shows pending issues count per team

**`--cleanup` flag** (lines 2415-2418)
- For each repo in config: call `cleanup_worktrees()`
- `cleanup_worktrees()` (lines 599-633):
  - Checks PR state for each worktree's branch
  - If PR is MERGED or CLOSED: remove worktree and branch
  - Prune stale worktrees: `git worktree prune`
  - Clean up agent DB containers

**`--review-pr <num> --repo <path>`** (lines 2392-2413)
- Standalone PR review (useful for manual PRs)
- Gets branch name from PR number
- Calls `auto_review_pr()` directly
- Outputs verdict

---

### **SUMMARY TABLE: Key Functions by Flow**

| Flow | Primary Function | Key Helper Functions | Line Range |
|------|------------------|----------------------|------------|
| 1. Polling | `run_agents_for_team()` | `fetch_agent_issues()` | 1691-2322, 522-595 |
| 2. Worktree | `run_agents_for_team()` | (inline) | 1809-1819 |
| 3. Claude invocation | `run_agents_for_team()` | (claude CLI) | 1978-2049, 1889-1896, 1936-1939 |
| 4. CI gate | `run_agents_for_team()` | `/bin/ci-gate` | 2098-2193 |
| 5. PR creation | `run_agents_for_team()` | `gather_pr_feedback()` | 2211-2275 |
| 6. Auto-review/fix | `auto_review_pr()`, `auto_fix_loop()` | `gather_pr_feedback()` | 679-798, 802-904 |
| 7. Orchestrator | `orchestrate_issue()` | `linear_create_sub_issue()`, `run_scoped_worker()` | 931-1357, 1360-1413 |
| 8. Resume | `run_agents_for_team()` | `linear_get_comments()`, `gather_pr_feedback()` | 1879-1941 |
| 9. Spec | `run_spec()` | (Claude skill) | 1603-1687 |
| 10. Pipeline | `run_pipeline()` | `linear_graphql()` | 1417-1599 |
| 11. Slack | `slack_notify()` | `resolve_slack_webhook()` | 312-318, 358-379 |
| 12. Blocking | `run_agents_for_team()` | (agent-written file check) | 2065-2082 |

---

### **DATA FLOW SUMMARY**

```
Linear Issue (with "agent" label)
    ↓
fetch_agent_issues() → GraphQL query
    ↓
run_agents_for_team() [main loop]
    ├─→ Create/reuse worktree
    ├─→ Export CLAUDE_AGENT_* env vars
    ├─→ Start DB container (if configured)
    └─→ Spawn Claude subprocess with --permission-mode bypassPermissions
        ├─→ Standard: Claude implements in isolated worktree
        ├─→ Orchestrator: Claude decomposes issue, creates sub-issues
        │   └─→ Scoped workers: Each subtask gets Claude with scope enforcement
        └─→ Resume: Claude continues from saved session with feedback
            ├─→ Blocked: Posts question to Linear, waits for reply
            └─→ Feedback: Addresses PR review comments
    ├─→ Check for .claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md
    │   └─→ If exists: Post to Linear, exit (wait for developer response)
    ├─→ Run CI gate (ci-gate tool)
    │   └─→ If fails: Spawn Claude fix agent, retry
    ├─→ If CI passes: git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
    ├─→ Create/update PR via gh pr create
    ├─→ Post implementation report to Linear
    ├─→ [Optional] auto_review_pr() → spawn review Claude
    │   └─→ If "request_changes": auto_fix_loop() → spawn fix agent → re-review (up to N iterations)
    └─→ Post to Slack webhook

Database: Agent tracks state in JSONL trace files (~/.config/claude-agents/traces/)
```

---

This comprehensive mapping covers all 12 lifecycle flows with exact line numbers, key functions, environment variables, and integration points. Every major decision point, error handling path, and notification trigger is documented.

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me compile my comprehensive findings into a structured report:

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: claude-agent-runner Lifecycle

I've thoroughly explored the codebase. Here's the complete breakdown of all 12 flows you requested:

---

### **1. MAIN LOOP / POLLING LOGIC**

**Function: `run_agents_for_team()` (lines 1691-2322)**

The main polling loop:
1. **Lock management** (lines 1703-1714): Checks per-repo lock file to prevent parallel runs; uses process PID
2. **Cleanup** (line 1718): Calls `cleanup_worktrees()` to remove merged PRs
3. **Slot counting** (lines 1720-1746): Tracks active agents by reading `.claude/agent.pid` files in worktrees; calculates available slots based on `MAX_PARALLEL` config
4. **Issue fetching** (lines 1750-1758): Calls `fetch_agent_issues()` with the API key, team name, and available slots
5. **Per-issue loop** (lines 1770-2319): For each issue:
   - Extracts ID, identifier, title, description
   - Generates branch name and worktree path
   - Detects resume vs. new issue (lines 1795-1819)
   - Spawns agent subprocess in background (line 2314) with PID tracking

**Helper: `fetch_agent_issues()` (lines 522-595)**
- Uses Linear GraphQL query to find issues with configurable label (default: "agent")
- Queries: id, identifier, title, description, team, parent, labels, state
- **With --sequenced flag**: Also fetches relations to filter out issues with incomplete blockers (lines 586-591)
- Returns array of issues up to `LIMIT` (available slots)

**Entry point for polling (line 2337-2346)**:
```bash
case "${1:-}" in
  --all|"") 
    # Default: iterate all workspaces/teams from config.json
    # Calls run_agents_for_team for each
```

---

### **2. WORKTREE CREATION FLOW**

**Location: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 1809-1819**

**Full flow:**
1. **Detect resume** (lines 1795-1806):
   - If worktree already exists, check for `.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md` → resume reason = "blocked"
   - Otherwise → resume reason = "feedback" (PR feedback resume)
   - Trace event: `worktree.reused`

2. **Create new worktree** (lines 1809-1819):
   ```bash
   git fetch origin main
   git worktree add "$WORKTREE" -b "$BRANCH" origin/main
   ```
   - Worktree path: `~/.claude/worktrees/${REPO_NAME}/issue-${IDENTIFIER}/`
   - Branch: `agent/${IDENTIFIER}-${TITLE_SLUG}`
   - Copy `.env` and `.env.local` from repo root (lines 1816-1817)
   - Trace event: `worktree.created`

3. **Update Linear state** (lines 1822-1823):
   - Call `linear_set_state()` → "In Progress"
   - Call `linear_remove_label()` → Remove the "agent" label

4. **Start DB container if configured** (lines 1826-1828):
   - Calls `start_agent_db()` (see flow #5)

5. **Spawn agent subprocess** (lines 1832-2316):
   - Subshell: `(...) >> "$ISSUE_LOG" 2>&1 &`
   - Records PID to `$WORKTREE/.claude/agent.pid`

---

### **3. CLAUDE INVOCATION (SPAWNING)**

**Location: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 1832-2049 (standard), 1879-1941 (resume), 1947-2052 (orchestrator/scoped)**

**Environment exports:**
```bash
export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1              # Enables guardrail hooks
export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO="$REPO_NAME"
export CLAUDE_AGENT_BRANCH="$BRANCH"
export CLAUDE_AGENT_ISSUE_ID="$IDENTIFIER"
export CLAUDE_AGENT_WORKTREE="$WORKTREE"
unset CLAUDECODE                         # Allow spawning Claude from within Claude
```

**Standard single-agent invocation** (lines 1978-2049):
```bash
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
  --setting-sources project,local \
  -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
Read CLAUDE.md for all project conventions...
## Linear Issue: ${IDENTIFIER}
**Title:** ${TITLE}
**Description:** ${DESCRIPTION}
..." \
  --max-turns 500
```

**Resume: Blocked question** (lines 1889-1896):
```bash
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
  --setting-sources project,local \
  -r "$PREV_SESSION" \  # Resume from saved session ID
  -p "The developer has responded to your question. Here are the latest comments from Linear:
${COMMENTS}
Continue implementing ${IDENTIFIER}..." \
  --max-turns 500
```

**Resume: Feedback on PR** (lines 1936-1939):
```bash
claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
  --setting-sources project,local \
  [-r "$PREV_SESSION"] \
  -p "## Feedback Resume: ${IDENTIFIER} — ${TITLE}
Reviewers have left feedback on your PR and/or the Linear ticket. Address ALL feedback below..."
```

**Session ID persistence** (lines 1876-1882, 2055-2063):
- Saves session ID to `$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-session.id`
- Reads from `~/.claude/projects/<worktree-escaped>/` latest .jsonl file
- Used for resuming across feedback cycles

---

### **4. CI GATE / RETRY LOOP**

**Location: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 2084-2193**

**Flow:**
1. **Check commits** (lines 2085-2096):
   - Count: `git rev-list --count origin/main..HEAD`
   - If 0 commits → exit with warning; no push/PR created

2. **Check blocking file** (lines 2066-2082):
   - If `.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md` exists → post to Linear, set state to "Todo", exit
   - Trace event: `agent.blocked`

3. **CI gate retry loop** (lines 2102-2193):
   - **Attempt count**: 1 to `MAX_CI_RETRIES` (default 3)
   - **Each attempt**:
     - Call `$CI_GATE "$WORKTREE"` (see `/bin/ci-gate` at the end)
     - Log output to `$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-ci-${ATTEMPT}.log`
     - Trace event: `ci.passed` or `ci.failed`
     - Post status comment to Linear (lines 2104)
   
   - **If CI fails** (lines 2113-2142):
     - First 2 attempts (< MAX_CI_RETRIES):
       - Extract error output
       - Spawn Claude fix agent (lines 2123-2142):
         ```bash
         claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
           --setting-sources project,local \
           -p "## CI Gate Failed — Fix These Errors
         ${CI_ERRORS}
         ..." --max-turns 500
         ```
       - Trace event: `ci_fix.started` / `ci_fix.finished`
       - Loop continues
     
     - Final failure (attempt == MAX_CI_RETRIES):
       - Build failure report from all CI logs (lines 2159-2185)
       - Post to Linear as comment with report
       - Post to Slack
       - Trace event: none (logged as exit)
       - Exit early

4. **If CI passes** (lines 2195-2277):
   - Clean up transient status comments
   - Post agent report to Linear if exists (lines 2202-2204)
   - Push branch: `git push -u origin "$BRANCH"`
   - Trace event: `pr.pushed`

**ci-gate tool** (`/bin/ci-gate` lines 1-100+):
- Detects project type: TypeScript/Next.js, Rust, Python, Go
- Runs: `tsc --noEmit`, tests (vitest/jest/pytest), build (next/vite/cargo/go)
- Can use project-specific `.ci-gate` file with custom commands
- Exit 0 = pass, Exit 1 = fail

### **5. PR CREATION & POSTING FLOW**

**Location: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 2211-2275**

**Two paths: feedback update vs. new PR**

**Path A: Feedback-addressed PR (lines 2213-2228)**:
- Check if existing PR: `gh pr view "$BRANCH"`
- If exists:
  - Set Linear state to "In Review"
  - Post comment: "✅ **Feedback addressed** — new commits pushed to existing PR: ${EXISTING_PR}"
  - Post to Slack
  - If `AUTO_REVIEW=true`: Call `auto_review_pr()` (see flow #6)
  - If `AUTO_FIX=true` AND verdict is "request_changes": Call `auto_fix_loop()` (see flow #6)

**Path B: New PR creation** (lines 2229-2275):
1. **Build PR body** (lines 2230-2245):
   - Check for `.claude/agent-reports/${IDENTIFIER}.md` (implementation report)
   - If exists: use as PR body
   - If not: use issue title + description
   - Append footer: "*Implemented by autonomous Claude agent. CI gate passed: type-check, tests, build.*"
   - Close with `Closes ${IDENTIFIER}`

2. **Create draft PR** (lines 2247-2252):
   ```bash
   gh pr create \
     --draft \
     --title "${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}" \
     --body "$PR_BODY" \
     --head "$BRANCH" \
     --base main
   ```
   - Trace event: `pr.created url=$PR_URL`

3. **Update Linear & Slack** (lines 2254-2260):
   - Set state: "In Review"
   - Post comment: "✅ **Agent completed** — CI gate passed, PR opened: ${PR_URL}"
   - Post to Slack
   - Log to issue log

4. **Auto-review (if enabled)** (lines 2264-2270):
   - Same as feedback path

### **6. AUTO-REVIEW & AUTO-FIX CYCLES**

**Auto-review: `auto_review_pr()` (lines 679-798)**

**Flow:**
1. **Fetch PR info**:
   - Get PR number from branch
   - Get full diff: `gh pr diff "$BRANCH"`
   - Get title and body

2. **Spawn review agent** (line 739):
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode plan --print \
     -p "You are a senior code reviewer. Review this PR thoroughly.
   [Full PR title, description, and diff]
   
   Output ONLY a JSON object with this exact structure:
   {
     \"verdict\": \"approve\" | \"request_changes\" | \"comment\",
     \"summary\": \"...\",
     \"blocking\": [...],
     \"important\": [...],
     \"nits\": [...],
     \"praise\": [...]
   }" --max-turns 20
   ```
   - Mode: `--permission-mode plan` (read-only, safe)
   - Expected output: JSON with structured review

3. **Parse review** (lines 749-794):
   - Extract verdict, summary, blocking/important/nits/praise
   - If JSON valid:
     - Format as markdown
     - Post via `gh pr review` with verdict (approve/request-changes/comment)
   - If non-JSON:
     - Post as plain comment
   - Trace event: `review.completed verdict=$VERDICT pr_number=$PR_NUMBER`

4. **Return verdict** (line 797):
   - Echo "approve" | "request_changes" | "comment"

**Auto-fix loop: `auto_fix_loop()` (lines 802-904)**

**Triggered if:** `AUTO_REVIEW=true` AND review verdict = "request_changes"

**Iteration loop** (lines 814-900, up to `MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS` — default 3):

1. **Check latest review state** (lines 820-830):
   - `gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json reviews`
   - If latest state == "APPROVED" → exit loop
   - If latest state != "CHANGES_REQUESTED" → exit loop

2. **Gather feedback** (lines 833-839):
   - Call `gather_pr_feedback()` (see below)
   - If empty → exit loop

3. **Spawn fix agent** (lines 842-862):
   ```bash
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO / BRANCH / ISSUE_ID / WORKTREE
   
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree for the ${REPO_NAME} project.
   
   ## Fix PR Feedback: ${IDENTIFIER}
   
   Address ALL reviewer feedback below. Make new, atomic commits for each fix.
   ${PR_FEEDBACK}
   ..." --max-turns 500
   ```
   - Trace event: `fix.started iteration=$ITER`

4. **Run CI gate on fixes** (lines 867-882):
   - Same retry logic as standard flow
   - If fails: spawn one inline Claude retry (lines 871-875)
   - If still fails: exit loop
   - Trace event: `fix.ci_failed iteration=$ITER` → return 1

5. **Push fixes & re-review** (lines 886-900):
   - `git push origin "$BRANCH"`
   - Call `auto_review_pr()` again
   - If verdict == "approve" → exit loop successfully (trace: `fix.completed`)
   - Otherwise: continue to next iteration

6. **Loop exhaustion** (lines 901-903):
   - Log: "Exhausted ${MAX_REVIEW_FIX_ITERATIONS} fix iterations"
   - Return 1

**Helper: `gather_pr_feedback()` (lines 262-308)**

Collects all feedback:
1. **PR top-level comments**: `gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json comments`
2. **Review body comments**: `gh pr view "$BRANCH" --json reviews`
3. **Inline file comments**: `gh api repos/${OWNER_REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments`

Returns markdown-formatted feedback with author, file:line, and comment body.

### **7. ORCHESTRATOR FLOW**

**Main entry: `orchestrate_issue()` (lines 931-1357)**

**Triggered by:**
- `ORCH_ENABLED=true` in config
- Issue has `Orchestrate` label (checked by `should_orchestrate()` at line 909-918)
- Called from `run_agents_for_team()` at line 1952

**5-phase orchestration process:**

**PHASE 1: Decomposition (lines 955-983)**
1. Create `.claude/orchestrator/` directory in worktree
2. Export `CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1` and agent env vars
3. Spawn orchestrator Claude (lines 962-983):
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     -p "You are an orchestrator decomposing a Linear issue into subtasks.
   
   ## Parent Issue: ${IDENTIFIER}
   **Title:** ${TITLE}
   **Description:** ${DESCRIPTION}
   
   ## Instructions
   Use the orchestrate skill to decompose this issue into scoped subtasks.
   Write subtask JSON files to .claude/orchestrator/subtask-NNN.json
   
   If this issue is single-concern and doesn't need decomposition, write:
   {\"no_decomposition\": true, \"reason\": \"...\"}
   to .claude/orchestrator/no_decomposition.json
   
   Max subtasks: ${ORCH_MAX_SUBTASKS}
   ..." --max-turns "$ORCH_MAX_TURNS"
   ```
   - Trace event: `orchestrator.fallback reason=no_decomposition` or `orchestrator.decomposed count=$SUBTASK_COUNT`

4. **Check fallback conditions** (lines 985-1011):
   - If `no_decomposition.json` exists → return 1 (fall back to single agent)
   - If subtask count < 2 → return 1

**PHASE 2: Create sub-issues in Linear (lines 1024-1084)**

For each `subtask-NNN.json` file:
1. Extract: id, title, skill, scope, off_limits, shared_files, acceptance_criteria, context, depends_on
2. Build sub-issue description with scope metadata (lines 1038-1058):
   ```
   ## Subtask of ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}
   ${ST_CONTEXT}
   
   ## File Scope
   **Allowed (write):** ${ST_SCOPE}
   **Off limits:** ...
   **Shared files (read-only):** ...
   
   ## Acceptance Criteria
   ${ST_CRITERIA}
   
   ## Dependencies
   ${ST_DEPS}
   ```

3. Create via `linear_create_sub_issue()` (lines 1062-1063):
   - Mutation: issueCreate with parentId=ISSUE_ID, add agent label
   - Track in STATE_SUBTASKS array (lines 1074-1079)
   - Trace event: `subtask.created subtask_id=$ST_ID identifier=$SUB_IDENTIFIER`

4. **Create dependency relations** (lines 1085-1116):
   - For each subtask with `depends_on[]`:
     - Remove agent label (won't get picked until unblocked)
     - Call `issueRelationCreate` with type="isBlockedBy"

5. **Write state.json** (lines 1122-1136):
   ```json
   {
     "parent_id": "${IDENTIFIER}",
     "branch": "agent/${IDENTIFIER}",
     "issue_id": "${ISSUE_ID}",
     "subtasks": [{"id": "ST-1", "linear_id": "...", "status": "pending", "skill": "..."}],
     "shared_files": [...],
     "created_at": "...",
     "status": "active"
   }
   ```

6. **Commit and notify** (lines 1155-1153):
   - `git add .claude/orchestrator/ && git commit`
   - Post to Linear: subtask summary with links
   - Trace event: orchestrator state saved

**PHASE 3: Supervisor loop (lines 1161-1234)**

Polls every `ORCH_POLL_INTERVAL` seconds (default 30) until all subtasks done:

1. Read state.json
2. For each non-completed subtask:
   - Query Linear: `linear_get_issue_state()` → type (completed/canceled/pending/todo/etc)
   - If type == "completed":
     - Check for scope-overflow file: `.claude/orchestrator/scope-overflow-${ST_ID}.md`
     - If overflow exists: post to Linear about follow-up
     - Update STATE_SUBTASKS status → "completed"
     - Trace event: `subtask.completed subtask_id=$ST_ID`
   - If type == "canceled":
     - Update status → "canceled"
   - Otherwise: ALL_DONE=false

3. Update state.json with new subtask statuses
4. If ALL_DONE → break loop

**PHASE 4: Integration validation (lines 1239-1290)**

1. Pull latest changes from subtask workers:
   - `git pull origin "agent/${IDENTIFIER}"`

2. Run CI gate on integrated result (lines 1244-1290):
   - If passes → INTEGRATION_PASSED=true, trace: `integration.passed`
   - If fails:
     - Extract error output
     - Spawn fix agent (lines 1262-1276):
       ```bash
       claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
         --setting-sources project,local \
         -p "This is an orchestrated feature (${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}) with multiple subtasks...
       ## Integration CI Failed — Fix These Errors
       [errors]
       ..." --max-turns 500
       ```
     - Retry CI (lines 1279-1286)
     - Trace: `integration.failed` or `integration.passed after_fix=true`

**PHASE 5: Create combined PR (lines 1292-1356)**

1. Push: `git push -u origin "agent/${IDENTIFIER}"`

2. Build PR body from all subtask reports (lines 1299-1320):
   ```
   ## ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE}
   
   ### Orchestrated Implementation
   This feature was decomposed into ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks...
   
   ### Subtask Reports
   [each subtask's .claude/agent-reports/${ST_IDENT}.md]
   
   Closes ${IDENTIFIER}
   ```

3. Create PR via `gh pr create` (lines 1327-1333)

4. **Success path** (lines 1335-1343):
   - Set Linear state to "In Review"
   - Post: "✅ **Orchestrator completed** — all ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks done, CI passed, PR: ${PR_URL}"
   - Update state.json status → "completed"
   - Return 0

5. **Failure path** (lines 1344-1349):
   - Post: "❌ **Orchestrator integration CI failed** — needs human intervention"
   - Return 0 (don't crash main flow)

**Scoped worker: `run_scoped_worker()` (lines 1360-1413)**

Called when issue has parent (orchestrated subtask):

1. **Extract scope from description** (lines 1367-1380):
   - Parse "## File Scope" section for "Allowed (write):" line
   - Extract comma-separated paths

2. **Create scope file** (lines 1375-1383):
   - Path: `.claude/orchestrator/active-scope.json`
   - Format: `{"scope": ["path/a", "path/b"]}`

3. **Set scoped environment** (lines 1386-1391):
   ```bash
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPED=1
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_SCOPE_FILE="$SCOPE_FILE"
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_MODE=1
   export CLAUDE_AGENT_REPO / BRANCH / ISSUE_ID / WORKTREE
   ```

4. **Spawn scoped worker Claude** (lines 1395-1409):
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     -p "You are a scoped worker in an isolated git worktree...
   
   ## Your Subtask: ${IDENTIFIER} — ${TITLE}
   
   Use the scoped-worker skill for your implementation workflow.
   
   [Full subtask description with scope/criteria/deps]
   
   ## IMPORTANT
   - Your file writes are enforced by the scope-guard hook
   - If you need out-of-scope changes, write to .claude/orchestrator/scope-overflow
   ..." --max-turns 500
   ```
   - Trace event: `agent.started mode=scoped-worker`

### **8. FEEDBACK/RESUME FLOW**

**Entry: `run_agents_for_team()` lines 1793-1942**

**Automatic detection:**
- If worktree already exists: RESUMING=true
- Check for `.claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md`:
  - Yes → RESUME_REASON="blocked"
  - No → RESUME_REASON="feedback"

**Resume blocked** (lines 1882-1900):
1. Read new comments from Linear: `linear_get_comments()`
2. Post: "🔄 **Agent resuming** — developer responded, continuing work"
3. Spawn Claude with -r flag (resume session):
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     -r "$PREV_SESSION" \
     -p "The developer has responded to your question. Here are the latest comments:
   ${COMMENTS}
   
   Continue implementing ${IDENTIFIER}..."
   ```
4. Trace: `issue.resumed reason=blocked`

**Resume feedback** (lines 1902-1941):
1. Gather PR and Linear feedback: `gather_pr_feedback()` + `linear_get_comments()`
2. Post: "🔄 **Agent resuming** — addressing reviewer feedback"
3. Build feedback prompt with all comments
4. Spawn Claude:
   ```bash
   claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions \
     --setting-sources project,local \
     [-r "$PREV_SESSION"] \
     -p "You are working in an isolated git worktree...
   
   ## Feedback Resume: ${IDENTIFIER} — ${TITLE}
   
   Reviewers have left feedback on your PR and/or the Linear ticket. Address ALL feedback below with new commits.
   
   [PR Feedback section]
   [Linear Ticket Comments section]
   
   Address every piece of feedback. If unclear, write to .claude/agent-blocked/${IDENTIFIER}.md"
   ```
5. Trace: `issue.resumed reason=feedback`

**Persistence mechanism** (lines 2055-2063):
- After Claude finishes, extract session ID from `~/.claude/projects/<worktree-escaped>/<latest>.jsonl`
- Save to `$LOG_DIR/${WORKSPACE}-${IDENTIFIER}-session.id`
- On next resume, read session ID and use with `-r` flag

**Triggering resume:**
- Developer re-adds the "agent" label to the issue in Linear
- Next `run_agents_for_team()` poll picks it up
- System detects existing worktree and resumes

### **9. --SPEC FLOW END-TO-END**

**Entry: `run_spec()` (lines 1603-1687)**

**Command invocation:**
```bash
claude-agent-runner --spec <idea.md> [--workspace <name>] [--team <name>] [--interactive] [--create]
```

**Flow:**

1. **Load idea file** (line 1616):
   - Read markdown file with freeform idea

2. **Resolve workspace/team** (lines 1619-1624):
   - Default to first in config if not specified

3. **Load project context** (lines 1632-1642):
   - Read project CLAUDE.md if repo available
   - Include in spec prompt for context-aware generation

4. **Choose mode** (lines 1658-1686):

   **Interactive mode** (`--interactive` flag):
   - Spawn Claude in interactive mode:
     ```bash
     claude --permission-mode plan \
       --setting-sources project,local \
       -p "Use the spec-writer skill to transform this idea into structured development requirements.
     
     ## Project Context (from CLAUDE.md)
     ${CLAUDE_MD}
     
     ## Idea
     ${IDEA}
     
     ## Output Format
     Output a markdown document compatible with the --pipeline command:
     - Each story has a title, estimate, labels, dependencies, and description
     - Stories should be sized for single agent sessions (1-3 hours)
     - Include acceptance criteria for each story
     - Note which stories should get the 'Orchestrate' label"
     ```
   - User can ask questions interactively

   **Non-interactive mode** (default):
   - Spawn Claude with --max-turns 20:
     ```bash
     claude --print --permission-mode plan \
       --setting-sources project,local \
       -p "[same prompt as above]" \
       --max-turns 20
     ```
   - Claude generates spec in one shot
   - Output printed to stdout

5. **Chain to pipeline** (`--create` flag):
   - Save spec output to temp file
   - Call `run_pipeline()` (see flow #10) with that file
   - Clean up temp file

**Output format:**
- Markdown compatible with `--pipeline`
- Each story:
  ```
  - **Title**: Short imperative
  - **Estimate**: 1-8 story points
  - **Labels**: feature/bug/chore/improvement
  - **Dependencies**: [list of other story titles]
  - **Description**: Detailed description with acceptance criteria
  ```

### **10. --PIPELINE FLOW END-TO-END**

**Entry: `run_pipeline()` (lines 1417-1599)**

**Command invocation:**
```bash
claude-agent-runner --pipeline <requirements.md> [--workspace <name>] [--team <name>] [--dry-run]
```

**Flow:**

1. **Load requirements file** (lines 1428-1429):
   - Read markdown containing structured requirements

2. **Resolve workspace/team** (lines 1431-1437):
   - Default to first in config if not specified

3. **Spawn parser Claude** (lines 1445-1469):
   ```bash
   claude --print --max-turns 3 --output-format json \
     -p "Parse the following product requirements into structured development stories.
   
   Output ONLY a JSON array with this structure:
   [
     {
       \"title\": \"Short imperative title\",
       \"description\": \"Detailed description with acceptance criteria\",
       \"estimate\": 1,
       \"labels\": [\"feature\"],
       \"dependencies\": []
     }
   ]
   
   Rules:
   - estimate is in story points (1-8)
   - labels: use 'feature', 'bug', 'chore', 'improvement'
   - dependencies: list titles of other stories this depends on
   - Break large requirements into small, independent stories
   - Each story completable in 1-3 hours
   - Output ONLY the JSON array, nothing else
   
   [requirements]"
   ```

4. **Validate JSON** (lines 1476-1481):
   - Parse output with jq
   - Exit if invalid

5. **Dry-run mode** (`--dry-run`):
   - Pretty-print stories with estimate, labels, dependencies, description
   - Exit (no Linear issue creation)

6. **Get team/label IDs** (lines 1498-1515):
   - Query Linear for team ID (by name)
   - Query Linear for agent label ID (by name)
   - Warn if label not found

7. **Create issues** (lines 1523-1569):
   - For each story in parsed JSON:
     - Create via `linear_graphql()` issueCreate mutation
     - Set: teamId, title, description, estimate, labelIds
     - **Conditional labeling** (lines 1531-1543):
       - If `--sequenced` flag AND story has dependencies:
         - Don't add agent label (wait for blocker completion)
       - Else:
         - Add agent label (ready for agent to pick up)
     - Track title → issue ID mapping (TITLE_TO_ID)
     - Trace event: issues created

8. **Create dependency relations** (lines 1571-1596):
   - Second pass through stories
   - For each story with dependencies:
     - Look up dependency story IDs via TITLE_TO_ID
     - Call `issueRelationCreate()` mutation with type="isBlockedBy"
     - Log "Linked: story_title blocked by dependency_title"

9. **Complete** (line 1598):
   - Log: "Pipeline complete — $STORY_COUNT issues created in ${WORKSPACE}/${TEAM_NAME}"

### **11. SLACK NOTIFICATION FLOW**

**Function: `slack_notify()` (lines 312-318)**

```bash
slack_notify() {
  local WEBHOOK_URL="$1"
  local MESSAGE="$2"
  [ -z "$WEBHOOK_URL" ] && return 0
  curl -s -X POST "$WEBHOOK_URL" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d "{\"text\": $(echo "$MESSAGE" | jq -Rs .)}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
}
```

**Integration points:**

1. **Resolver: `resolve_slack_webhook()` (lines 358-379)**
   - Check config for `workspaces[WORKSPACE].slack_webhook_env` env var name
   - Fallback: `SLACK_WEBHOOK_${WORKSPACE}` (uppercase)
   - Return empty string if not configured (silent no-op)

2. **Notification triggers** (all in `run_agents_for_team()`):

   | Event | Message | Line |
   |-------|---------|------|
   | Resume (blocked) | 🔄 Agent resuming — developer responded to blocked question | 1886 |
   | Resume (feedback) | 🔄 Agent resuming — addressing PR/Linear feedback | 1907 |
   | Orchestrator start | 🧩 Orchestrator starting for ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE} | 1950 |
   | Scoped worker start | 🔧 Scoped worker started ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE} | 1968 |
   | Standard agent start | 🔧 Agent started ${IDENTIFIER}: ${TITLE} | 1976 |
   | Agent blocked | 🚧 Agent blocked on ${IDENTIFIER} — needs human input | 2078 |
   | Feedback addressed | ✅ ${IDENTIFIER} feedback addressed — PR updated: ${EXISTING_PR} | 2218 |
   | PR created | ✅ ${IDENTIFIER} completed — PR opened: ${PR_URL} | 2260 |
   | PR creation failed | ❌ Push succeeded but PR creation failed | (N/A in notification) |
   | CI passed (no gate) | ✅ ${IDENTIFIER} completed (no CI gate) — PR: ${PR_URL} | 2298 |
   | Orchestrator completed | ✅ Orchestrated ${IDENTIFIER} completed — ${SUBTASK_COUNT} subtasks, PR: ${PR_URL} | 1338 |
   | Orchestrator CI failed | ❌ Orchestrated ${IDENTIFIER} integration CI failed — needs human intervention | 1348 |
